opinion.h: KAVANAGH v. STENHOUSE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF RHODE ISLAND. No. 615. Decided February 19, 1962.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
J. Joseph Nugent, Attorney General of Rhode Island, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS is of the opinion that further consideration of the question of jurisdiction should be postponed to a hearing of the case on the merits.
368 U.S. 516 RAINSBERGER v. LEYPOLDT, SHERIFF. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEVADA. No. 835, Misc. Decided February 19, 1962.
John F. Mendoza and Charles L. Garner for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
